category_sources: source_facts.description says dehydrated is a LetsEncrypt/ACME client implemented as a shell script; official README describes certificate signing with ACME servers.
display_name_sources: source_facts.name is dehydrated; official website and GitHub repository use dehydrated.
docs_sources: Official GitHub repository contains a docs directory, README getting-started/usage sections, and an official GitHub wiki.
repo_sources: source_facts.repo already has a value, so repo is returned as null per instruction.
tags_sources: source_facts.description and official README identify dehydrated as a shell ACME client for signing and renewing certificates.
